channel.title: GDG - Grafana Dash-n-Grab on GDG
channel.description: Recent content in GDG - Grafana Dash-n-Grab on GDG

item.title: Version 0.9
item.description: Release Notes for v0.9.0 Release Date: 01/08/2026 Min Recommended Grafana Versions: Grafana 11+ Release Notes: This was a pretty big change with it has a good bit of breaking changes.

item.title: Version 0.8
item.description: Release Notes for v0.8.1 Release Date: 06/30/2025 BugFix: #456 Issued with folders containing spaces Changes: #450 Update GoReleaser configurations (#453) Changes the patterns for brew installs from Formula to Cask: https://goreleaser.

item.title: Version 0.7
item.description: Release Notes for v0.7.2 Release Date: 01/17/2025 Breaking Changes #318 Only affects those with no valid configuration present. Removed the default config fall back.

item.title: Legacy Versions
item.description: Release Notes for v0.6.0 Release Date: 03/11/2024 Breaking Changes This is a major release, so once again doing some cleanups and introducing some breaking changes.

item.title: Getting Started
item.description: Setup new configuration You can create new context configuration using an interactive setup. $ gdg tools contexts new mycontext When creating a new context, you will be asked for authorization type, your default datasource and username/password, along with which folders you wish to manage under the context.

item.title: Installation
item.description: Installation The easiest way to install GDG is to get one of the pre-compiled binaries from our release page which can be found here.

item.title: Frequently Asked Questions
item.description: Can I use GDG to backup grafana? yes and no. GDG is not a full backup solution. If you are migrating from one server to another, doing disaster recovery gdg is not the right tool for the job.

item.title: Backup Guide
item.description: Every namespace supporting CRUD operations has the functions: list, download, upload, clear operating on only the monitored folders. Alerting Alerting is made up of several type of entities: ContactPoints, Alert Rules, Notification Policy and finally Templates.

item.title: Tools Guide
item.description: This guide focuses on the ’tools’ subcommand. Every command that isn’t specific to a CRUD operation falls under the tools command.

item.title: Enterprise Guide
item.description: The features listed below are for the enterprise edition of Grafana only. They will not work on the OSS version.
